input.target_query: how to solve search across docs is fragmented in b2b saas knowledge management
input.icp_segment_label: Distributed Engineering Lead at 200-500 dev shop
input.angle: How distributed engineering teams pick a KB when half the team writes in Markdown and half doesn't
input.audience: Engineering Directors at 200–500-person dev shops with a Markdown-vs-WYSIWYG factional split

structure.heading: The fragmented-search problem nobody admits to
structure.purpose: Establish recognition of the pain in the reader's own words.
key_points: Search returning archived/duplicate docs erodes trust faster than the migration cost
Bus-factor risk is the real cost — not the seat license

structure.heading: Markdown source of truth vs WYSIWYG: stop arbitrating, start funneling
structure.purpose: Defuse the factional fight by reframing it as a workflow question.
key_points: Both groups care about the same outcome: their writing surviving the system
Tooling that exports clean Markdown wins the long game

structure.heading: The four decision criteria engineering directors actually evaluate on
structure.purpose: Map criteria to evaluation activities the reader can run this week.
key_points: Real-time collab parity with Notion (paired-doc test)

structure.heading: The 30-day pilot script
structure.purpose: Give the reader a copy-pasteable plan they can socialize to their VP.

structure.heading: When to walk away — three failure modes to watch
structure.purpose: Trust-building section: name what does NOT work.
key_points: Tool sprawl: layering a 5th tool on top
Half-rolled-out: split-brain across teams kills adoption
Lead with bus-factor framing, not feature comparison
Cite the Markdown vs WYSIWYG factional split as a known problem
Use the language pattern 'single source of truth' verbatim in H2 or intro
Avoid the word 'tribal knowledge' in titles; use it in body only — overused in SERP
